directive.title: RFQ data model and API endpoints

auditor.summary: The generated code provides a solid foundation with comprehensive TypeScript types and Zod schemas, but is incomplete due to truncation. The critical missing components are the PUT endpoint (src/pages/api/rfqs/[id].ts) and the database query implementation (src/lib/db/rfq-queries.ts). The existing GET and POST handlers are well-structured with proper validation, error handling, caching headers, and performance monitoring. However, the build cannot be shipped without completing the missing files and addressing the security concerns around CORS configuration. The code demonstrates good understanding of the requirements but fails to deliver the complete implementation.

top_issues.description: PUT endpoint is completely missing. The build specification requires PUT /api/rfqs/[id] for updating RFQs, but this file was not generated (truncated). This is a direct violation of acceptance criterion #3.

Database query implementation is missing. The acceptance criteria require connection pooling and prepared statements, but the actual query functions (getRFQs, createRFQ, logPerformanceMetrics) are not implemented in the generated code.
Missing 404 error handling. The acceptance criteria require proper 404 responses, but the code only handles 400, 405, 409, and 500 errors. No 404 is returned for not-found resources.
